item.title: Great comment from a subscriber who commented in our Chat on his direct experience. Agentic AI is pretty difficult to implement at scale in production because (1) a bunch of new types of systems need
item.description: pGreat comment from a subscriber who commented in our Chat on his direct experience. br /br /Agentic AI is pretty difficult to implement at scale in production because (1) a bunch of new types of systems need to be built that have never existed before (orchestration /routing, context engineering, st

item.title: This is true as I have heard this from contacts in the Valley. Goes with my pinned post. The AI race is shifting from bigger models to cheaper, smarter systems https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/the-ai-r
item.description: pThis is true as I have heard this from contacts in the Valley. Goes with my pinned post. br /br /The AI race is shifting from bigger models to cheaper, smarter systems a href="https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/the-ai-race-is-shifting-from-bigger-models-to-cheaper-smarter-systems.html"cnbc.com/2026/07

item.title: Measuring Is Not Enough Anymore
item.description: pspanIs measuring general AI capabilities a good strategy to reduce AI existential risk, compared to other strategies? A disclaimer upfront: To answer that, one organization in particular will serve as an example, but the argument that we'll be making applies just as much or little across all of AI

item.title: theteatoast/local-vuln-research-pipeline: Fully local vulnerability research pipeline — 14B code-specialized LLM reviews every source file exhaustively. https://github.com/theteatoast/local-vuln-resea
item.description: ptheteatoast/local-vuln-research-pipeline: Fully local vulnerability research pipeline — 14B code-specialized LLM reviews every source file exhaustively. a href="https://github.com/theteatoast/local-vuln-research-pipeline"github.com/theteatoast/local…/a/phr /bLink/bbr /a href="https://github.com/the

item.title: Don’t bring an AI detector to a deepfake fight: proving reality through multimodal provenance
item.description: pspanEpistemic status: confident on the framing, speculative on the implementation/span/pblockquotepbspanTL;DR:/span/bspan Fake media detectors are on the losing end of an arms race. Instead of trying to spot fakes, we need to distrust images and videos by default, unless we can prove they're real.

item.title: A Simple Model of AI "Psychosis"
item.description: pspan"AI Psychosis" has gone from an evocative term /spanspanfor people undergoing extreme delusions, sometimes even culminating in suicide/spanspan, to the now colloquial insult for anyone who's just a little /spanispantoo/span/ispan into LLMs./span/ppspanWhen it's not just being overused, I think

item.title: The Termination Circuit (how reasoning models stop thinking).
item.description: pspanReasoning models since the dawn of o1 and R1 have a tendency to overthink. Despite a lot of work on early-exit methods and steering, open-weight and smaller reasoning models still produce long chains of thought before they answer. I worked on discovering how much of that thinking is required be

item.title: Plan A: Suggestions For Further Work
item.description: pspanYesterday we released /spana href="http://ai-2040.com/"spanAI 2040: Plan A/span/aspan, but there's lots of work left to do. /span/ppspanWe still have tons of uncertainty about the future of AI and the best strategies for how humanity can successfully chart a path through the intelligence explos

item.title: An Induction Head in Disguise: Chasing Grammar in a Character-Level Transformer
item.description: pispanConfident in the measurements — copying score, OV logits, attention patterns. More exploratory on the interpretations of what the head "knows," two of which I ended up disconfirming myself. My first mechanistic interpretability post; corrections welcome./span/i/ppspanFull analysis and code: /s

item.title: RT by @EMostaque: The future will not be defined by who builds the smartest AI. It will be defined by who builds the infrastructure people can trust. We are building that open infrastructure.

item.title: AI Coding: Do Security Risks Outweigh Productivity Gains?
item.description: AI coding tools cost $19-$200/month/user, but security scanning, remediation, and false positives add hidden costs. Are the productivity gains worth it?

item.description: pMolmoAct2 runs zero-shot on an SO-ARM101, no training required 🦾br /br /a href="https://nitter.net/allen_ai" title="Ai2"@allen_ai/a open Action Reasoning Model is now in LeRobot with the full lifecycle covered: fine-tuning (full or LoRA), evaluation, and real-robot deployment. It pairs a Molmo2-ER

item.title: AI Surveillance and Social Progress
item.description: pIn the near future, a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/artificialintelligenceai"AI/a-powered surveillance systems will be able to track everything we do in public, and much of what we do in private. And if we do something wrong&#8212;shoplift, litter, jaywalk, you name it&#8212;the syst

item.title: RT by @ylecun: It's incredible to me that 9 months ago we didnt have an office, and now we have a fully functional humanoid and an efficient AI! So proud of the team 🤠
item.description: pIt's incredible to me that 9 months ago we didnt have an office, and now we have a fully functional humanoid and an efficient AI! So proud of the team 🤠/phr /blockquotebRemi Cadene (@RemiCadene)/bppStarting with the fundamentals br /br /Prototype Version 0br /AI, Software, Hardwarebr /A small team

item.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
item.description: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

item.title: HSBC Issues First Digitally Native Structured Product in Hong Kong
item.description: The bank placed USD-denominated notes issued directly on a blockchain, with Marketnode acting as tokenization agent and digital paying agent.

item.title: How robust are natural language autoencoders to initialization?
item.description: pNatural language autoencoders are meant to take in an LLM's activation vector and describe in plain text what the model is thinking. However, its training data collection involves asking Claude to guess what a model might be thinking. How robust are NLAs to these guesses? We change Claude's guesses

item.title: AI Agents Are a New Kind of Identity — and Most Organizations Aren't Ready
item.description: If you're handling AI agents like a service account or API token, consider yourself behind. AI agents need a fundamentally different approach.

Across the week, join live sessions and community events with builders around the world.br /br /Registration is open.br /Bring the idea sitting in your backlog.br /Build it with Codex./pa href="https://nitter.net/OpenAIDevs/status/2075

item.title: RT by @sama: Massive day for us @OpenAI: - GPT-5.6 SOTA at ~everything & by far most token efficient - Agents for everyone in the new ChatGPT app Work and Codex modes - Work mode available on desktop
